attacking_style: Not an attacker; pure leadout specialist who executes high-speed accelerations in final 1km, typically peeling off 180-200m from finish.
team_tactics: Positioned as penultimate leadout rider, delivering final sprinter with massive accelerations through technical sections (tunnels, bends) starting earlier than rival trains to create separation.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where sustained high-speed leadout work is required; particularly effective through technical features like tunnels that allow team to create selections.
vulnerability: Tactical positioning errors when peeling off on bends (right-side peel-off on right bend cost sprinter inside line); reliant on having elite sprinter to convert his work.
key_patterns: Executes penultimate leadout role, peeling off 180-200m from finish line (2 observations)
Delivers through technical features using surging accelerations to create selections (2 observations)
Team initiates sprint trains earlier than rivals to establish control (2 observations)
Elite-level leadout execution often wasted without top sprinter positioned behind him (2 observations)
form_trajectory: unknown: switched teams between observations (Jumbo-Visma to Bahrain), role consistent but no recent data post-2019
